column8: There are lots of beautiful, large houses in Britain. Many of them_____________built hundreds of years ago.
column9: In the past, they__________owned by very rich families.
column10: Today, many of them___________owned by an organisation called the National Trust which____________created to look after them.
column11: The houses____________kept in perfect condition, and visitors___________allowed to look round them.
column12: Milk was_____________kept in the fridge, because they didn't have fridges!
column13: Washing machines____________only invented very recently, so washing____________done by hand.
column14: In some cases, the house____________still lived in today.
column15: Then this happens, visitors_____________only shown part of the house.
column16: The private room_____________kept closed to the public.
column17: These houses often have beautiful gardens, too. The gardens______________looked after by professional gardeners.
column18: Members of The National Trust_____________given a discount.
column19: This year, millions of people______________be given the chance to see what life in an old country house was like.
